project.name: sandcastle-incus
project.description: A system that provisions and manages multi-tenant, tenant-scoped Incus container/VM machines for AI and development workflows, with identity/auth, private tenant networking (Tailscale + DNS), and published HTTP routes.

nodes.name: Identity & Access
nodes.summary: The Auth App handles user authentication and machine identity. It runs GitHub OAuth web login, browser-assisted CLI Device Login that issues Incus certificate add tokens, allowlist/user management, and the Sandcastle OIDC Provider that mints short-lived Workload Identity Tokens for machines.

businessRules: Only allowlisted GitHub users may complete device login
Workload tokens are short-lived and signed per-tenant
First device login provisions the user's personal tenant
crossDomainInteractions: Provisions a personal Tenant on first login
Mints Workload Identity Tokens consumed by Machines

nodes.name: Tenant Lifecycle
nodes.summary: Manages the full lifecycle of a Tenant, which is an Incus project boundary with its own CA, DNS suffix, CIDR allocation and sidecar. Covers create/delete plus list and status derived from live Incus topology.

businessRules: Each tenant is an isolated Incus project with a dedicated CIDR
Tenant creation is idempotent and reuses existing projects
Deleting a tenant cleans up shares and detaches its network
crossDomainInteractions: Provisioned by Identity & Access on first login and by the Project Broker
Brings up sidecar Tailscale + DNS in Networking

nodes.name: Machine Lifecycle
nodes.summary: Creates, connects to, and controls Tenant machines (Incus containers/VMs), including private-IP allocation, device/mount assembly, start/stop/restart/delete, and enabling GCP workload-identity token exchange for a machine.

businessRules: Machines are resolved with unique-search semantics across projects
Each machine gets a free private IP within the project CIDR
Workload identity requires an approved device login
crossDomainInteractions: Obtains Workload Identity Tokens from Identity & Access
Relies on Networking & DNS for connectivity and host overrides

nodes.name: Networking & DNS
nodes.summary: Wires tenants and machines onto a user-selected Tailscale network and serves private tenant DNS. Covers tailnet enrollment/verification, the per-tenant split-DNS reconciler on the sc-dns sidecar, and client-side host configuration (local resolvers, Tenant CA trust, /etc/hosts overrides).

businessRules: Each tenant advertises tenant-scoped Tailscale tags
Split-DNS zones are rendered from live instance IPs and applied idempotently
Client resolvers and CA trust are installed per-tenant
crossDomainInteractions: Configured during Tenant creation via the sidecar

nodes.name: Public Routes
nodes.summary: Publishes HTTP routes for tenant machines through shared-infrastructure Caddy. A route request is validated with DNS proof-of-ownership, sent to the Route Broker over mutual TLS, authorized against the caller's project access, and applied to global route infrastructure.

businessRules: The public hostname must pass DNS proof-of-ownership
The Route Broker authorizes callers via their Incus client certificate
A principal may only mutate routes in projects it owns
crossDomainInteractions: Depends on shared Caddy from Infrastructure & Provisioning
Resolves target Machines from Machine Lifecycle

nodes.name: Infrastructure & Provisioning
nodes.summary: Bootstraps and operates the shared platform: operator install of shared v2 infrastructure from the Infrastructure Seed File, base/AI OCI image build and publish via the Image Builder appliance, the mTLS Project Broker that provisions tenants/projects, and Storage Shares between tenants.

businessRules: The seed file carries Auth Hostname, remote, CIDR pool and TLS material
Install refuses to clobber an existing deployment
The Project Broker authorizes callers by certificate fingerprint
crossDomainInteractions: Provisions Tenants on behalf of authorized principals
Provides the shared Caddy that Public Routes depend on

nodes.name: CLI Device Login
nodes.summary: Browser-assisted device authorization: the CLI creates a device login, the user authenticates via GitHub and is checked against the allowlist, a personal tenant is provisioned on first login, and an Incus certificate add token is issued and polled for.

nodes.name: Create device login
nodes.summary: Creates a new device-login authorization record with a user code.

nodes.name: GitHub OAuth authenticate
nodes.summary: Redirects to GitHub and handles the OAuth callback to identify the user.

nodes.name: Approve for allowlisted user
nodes.summary: Approves the pending device login only for an allowlisted user.

nodes.name: Provision personal tenant
nodes.summary: Provisions or reuses the user's personal tenant during device login.

nodes.name: Issue certificate add token
nodes.summary: Creates an Incus certificate add token scoped to the tenant for enrollment.

nodes.name: Poll for credentials
nodes.summary: Handles the device-login poll, returning pending or completed credentials.

nodes.name: Post-login host setup
nodes.summary: Enrolls the tenant remote and completes client host setup after login.

nodes.name: GitHub Web Login
nodes.summary: The Auth App web login flow: redirect to GitHub OAuth, exchange the callback code, check the allowlist and upsert the user, and establish a web session.

nodes.name: Redirect to GitHub OAuth
nodes.summary: Starts the GitHub OAuth flow by redirecting the user to authorize.

nodes.name: Handle OAuth callback
nodes.summary: Handles the GitHub callback and exchanges the code for the user identity.

nodes.name: Check allowlist & upsert user
nodes.summary: Checks the allowlist and creates or updates the user in the SQLite store.

nodes.name: Establish web session
nodes.summary: Establishes the authenticated web session for the user.

nodes.name: Issue Workload Identity Token
nodes.summary: The Sandcastle OIDC Provider issues a short-lived Workload Identity Token to a machine: workload identity is enabled and a runtime secret provisioned, the tenant OIDC signing key is ensured, the caller is authenticated, and a signed JWT is minted.

nodes.name: Enable machine workload identity
nodes.summary: Enables workload identity on a machine and provisions its runtime secret.

nodes.name: Ensure OIDC signing key
nodes.summary: Ensures an active encrypted OIDC signing key exists for the tenant.

nodes.name: Authenticate runtime secret
nodes.summary: Authenticates the calling machine via its Machine Runtime Secret.

nodes.name: Mint short-lived token
nodes.summary: Mints a signed short-lived workload identity token.

nodes.name: Sign JWT with tenant key
nodes.summary: Signs the JWT with the tenant's active OIDC signing key.

nodes.name: Create Tenant
nodes.summary: Orchestrates full v2 tenant provisioning: builds the create plan (CA, DNS, CIDR, cloud-init), ensures the bridge and Incus project, creates and configures the sidecar, installs its packages, and brings Tailscale up inside it.

nodes.name: Build v2 create plan
nodes.summary: Builds the complete v2 tenant plan: CA material, DNS config, CIDR allocation and cloud-init.

nodes.name: Ensure tenant bridge
nodes.summary: Ensures the tenant's v2 bridge network exists with the correct configuration.

nodes.name: Ensure Incus project
nodes.summary: Idempotently creates or updates the v2 Incus project with metadata and image ownership.

nodes.name: Create and start sidecar
nodes.summary: Creates and starts the tenant's v2 sidecar instance, waiting for it to run.

nodes.name: Install sidecar packages
nodes.summary: Installs required packages inside the sidecar so it can run DNS and Tailscale.

nodes.name: Configure sidecar networking/DNS
nodes.summary: Configures the sidecar's networking, DNS and services after installation.

nodes.name: Bring Tailscale up
nodes.summary: Brings Tailscale up inside the sidecar, parsing the tailnet IP and login URL.

nodes.name: Delete Tenant
nodes.summary: Removes all Incus resources belonging to a tenant: builds the delete plan, cleans up shares involving the tenant, deletes each project completely, and detaches the tenant network from the default profile.

nodes.name: Build delete plan
nodes.summary: Builds the plan describing which tenant projects and resources to remove.

nodes.name: Cleanup tenant shares
nodes.summary: Reconciles and removes all storage shares involving the tenant being deleted.

nodes.name: Delete projects completely
nodes.summary: Removes every resource inside each Incus project before deleting the project.

nodes.name: Detach tenant network
nodes.summary: Detaches the tenant network device from the default profile so it can be deleted.

nodes.name: Execute deletion
nodes.summary: Executes the deletion plan and clears the tenant network from the remote.

nodes.name: List & Status Tenant
nodes.summary: Reports tenant state from live Incus topology: lists tenants under the install prefix, reads the live topology, and computes status combining share health, Tailscale checks and topology-derived diagnostics.

nodes.name: List tenants for prefix
nodes.summary: Lists tenants under the install prefix by reading live Incus projects into summaries.

nodes.name: Read live topology
nodes.summary: Reads live Incus state into the tenant Topology snapshot.

nodes.name: Compute status
nodes.summary: Computes full tenant status combining share, Tailscale and topology checks.

nodes.name: Evaluate share health
nodes.summary: Evaluates storage-share offer/recipient state health across related tenants.

nodes.name: Derive topology checks
nodes.summary: Derives sidecar and diagnostic-file presence checks from the topology snapshot.

nodes.name: Create Machine
nodes.summary: Plans and creates a tenant machine: parses the reference and flags, resolves tenant/project, allocates a private IP, assembles devices/mounts, confirms, creates the v2 instance and waits for its IPv4.

nodes.name: Parse machine reference
nodes.summary: Parses the create command flags and machine reference within the tenant.

nodes.name: Resolve tenant/project
nodes.summary: Resolves the tenant and project for the planned machine.

nodes.name: Allocate private IP
nodes.summary: Allocates a free private IP within the project CIDR avoiding existing machines.

nodes.name: Assemble devices & mounts
nodes.summary: Constructs the Incus device map including network, home and workspace mounts.

nodes.name: Confirm plan
nodes.summary: Prompts the user to confirm the machine creation plan.

nodes.name: Create v2 instance
nodes.summary: Creates the v2 machine instance in the tenant project and starts it.

nodes.name: Wait for instance IPv4
nodes.summary: Polls the instance until it reports an IPv4 address or times out.

nodes.name: Connect to Machine
nodes.summary: Opens a session on a tenant machine: resolves the target reference, creates the machine if it does not yet exist, lists remote projects to resolve the tenant, and launches an Incus exec/shell.

nodes.name: Resolve target machine
nodes.summary: Resolves the parsed machine reference against the tenant summary.

nodes.name: Ensure machine exists
nodes.summary: Idempotently ensures the machine exists, creating it if absent.

nodes.name: List remote projects
nodes.summary: Lists projects on the remote to resolve the tenant project.

nodes.name: Launch Incus exec/shell
nodes.summary: Launches an Incus exec/shell session on the resolved machine.

nodes.name: Control Machine
nodes.summary: Applies a lifecycle action (start/stop/restart/delete) to a machine: builds the generic lifecycle command, resolves the machine, applies the plan against the Incus API, repairs networking, and waits for the state.

nodes.name: Build lifecycle command
nodes.summary: Builds a generic machine lifecycle command from an action template.

nodes.name: Resolve machine
nodes.summary: Resolves the target machine with cross-project unique-search semantics.

nodes.name: Apply lifecycle plan
nodes.summary: Executes the lifecycle plan by updating instance state and waiting for completion.

nodes.name: Repair machine network
nodes.summary: Ensures correct static IP and gateway, running an in-instance heal script.

nodes.name: Enable Machine Workload Identity
nodes.summary: Enables GCP workload-identity token exchange on a machine: builds the enable command, approves the workload device login, builds the in-guest workload files and token-helper script, and applies them to the machine.

nodes.name: Build workload enable command
nodes.summary: Builds the CLI command enabling workload identity for a machine.

nodes.name: Approve workload device login
nodes.summary: Approves the workload device login during enablement.

nodes.name: Build in-guest workload files
nodes.summary: Builds the in-guest files enabling GCP workload-identity token exchange.

nodes.name: Generate token-helper script
nodes.summary: Generates the in-guest script that mints tokens via the Machine Runtime Secret.

nodes.name: Apply to machine
nodes.summary: Applies the workload identity configuration to the target machine.

nodes.name: Enroll Tailnet
nodes.summary: Joins the host to the tenant's Tailscale network: advertises tenant tags and runs tailscale up, parses local status to confirm membership, verifies the login tailnet, and ensures tenant routing.

nodes.name: Advertise tags & up
nodes.summary: Runs tailscale up advertising the tenant-scoped tags.

nodes.name: Bring Tailscale up
nodes.summary: Wraps tailscale up and waits for the interface to come online.

nodes.name: Verify tailnet membership
nodes.summary: Parses local Tailscale status and confirms membership in the expected tailnet.

nodes.name: Verify login tailnet
nodes.summary: Verifies the host is joined to the expected tenant tailnet during login.

nodes.name: Ensure tenant routing
nodes.summary: Ensures tenant routing so traffic egresses via the tailnet.

nodes.name: Reconcile Tenant Split-DNS
nodes.summary: Reconciles per-tenant split-DNS for every v2 tenant: iterates live infra projects, extracts tenant instance IPs, renders the zone, normalizes the serial for idempotent comparison, and applies it on the sc-dns sidecar.

nodes.name: Iterate infra projects
nodes.summary: Iterates live infra projects to reconcile split-DNS for every v2 tenant.

nodes.name: Extract tenant instance IPs
nodes.summary: Extracts each tenant instance's IPv4 address within the tenant CIDR.

nodes.name: Render DNS zone
nodes.summary: Renders the zone from live instances for a single tenant.

nodes.name: Normalize zone serial
nodes.summary: Normalizes the SOA serial to a stable value for idempotent comparison.

nodes.name: Apply zone on sidecar
nodes.summary: Applies the rendered zone on the sc-dns sidecar.

nodes.name: Configure Client Host
nodes.summary: Configures the local host for a tenant: plans and installs the per-tenant DNS resolver, installs the Tenant CA into the local trust store, and adds /etc/hosts overrides for machine hostnames.

nodes.name: Plan local DNS resolver
nodes.summary: Builds a local DNS resolver plan for a v2 tenant from its DNS suffix and CIDR.

nodes.name: Install resolver
nodes.summary: Installs the per-tenant resolver configuration on the host.

nodes.name: Install Tenant CA
nodes.summary: Assembles and applies the plan placing the Tenant CA into the local trust store.

nodes.name: Add host overrides
nodes.summary: Plans and adds /etc/hosts overrides for machine hostnames.

nodes.name: Publish Public Route
nodes.summary: Requests a new public HTTP route: normalizes the hostname, builds the create plan and resolves the target machine, verifies DNS proof-of-ownership, sends the request to the Route Broker over mTLS, which authorizes it and mutates the shared route infrastructure.

nodes.name: Build route create command
nodes.summary: Builds the route create command that requests a new public route.

nodes.name: Normalize public hostname
nodes.summary: Validates and normalizes the user-supplied public route hostname.

nodes.name: Build create plan
nodes.summary: Builds the create plan including target machine resolution.

nodes.name: Verify DNS proof-of-ownership
nodes.summary: Verifies the hostname's DNS proof by resolving it and matching expected targets.

nodes.name: Send request over mTLS
nodes.summary: Sends the route-create request to the Route Broker over mutual TLS.

nodes.name: Authorize create
nodes.summary: Authorizes the route creation against the principal's project access.

nodes.name: Mutate route infrastructure
nodes.summary: Stamps the route creator and mutates the shared Caddy route infrastructure.

nodes.name: Delete Public Route
nodes.summary: Removes a public route: builds the delete command, sends the request over mTLS, maps the certificate fingerprint to a principal, authorizes deletion against the route's project, and removes the route from infrastructure.

nodes.name: Build route delete command
nodes.summary: Builds the route delete command that removes a public route.

nodes.name: Send delete over mTLS
nodes.summary: Sends the route-delete request to the Route Broker over mutual TLS.

nodes.name: Map fingerprint to principal
nodes.summary: Maps the client-certificate fingerprint to a route-broker principal.

nodes.name: Authorize delete
nodes.summary: Authorizes the deletion by checking the principal owns the route's project.

nodes.name: Remove route from infra
nodes.summary: Removes the route from the shared route infrastructure.

nodes.name: Install Shared Infrastructure
nodes.summary: Operator install of shared v2 infrastructure: builds the install command and detects an existing deployment, validates host ports and CIDR overlap, plans the shared infra, reads/writes the Infrastructure Seed File, and bootstraps the Auth App and broker appliances.

nodes.name: Build install command
nodes.summary: Constructs the admin install command that plans and executes shared v2 infrastructure.

nodes.name: Validate host ports & CIDR
nodes.summary: Checks required host ports and that the CIDR pool does not overlap host interfaces.

nodes.name: Plan shared infra
nodes.summary: Plans shared infrastructure creation for the deployment prefix.

nodes.name: Read/write seed file
nodes.summary: Reads or writes the Infrastructure Seed File bootstrap bundle.

nodes.name: Bootstrap Auth App
nodes.summary: Bootstraps the Auth App appliance from the plan.

nodes.name: Bootstrap brokers
nodes.summary: Bootstraps the Route and Project Broker appliances.

nodes.name: Install & init Incus
nodes.summary: Generates and runs the script installing and initializing Incus for the OS suite.

nodes.name: Build & Publish Image
nodes.summary: Builds and publishes base/AI OCI images: plans the image build, plans and provisions the Image Builder appliance, plans the remote build with the GHCR target, runs the build script, and syncs/imports the image into the remote.

nodes.name: Plan image build
nodes.summary: Builds the image build plan resolving repo root, build command and template alias.

nodes.name: Plan builder appliance
nodes.summary: Plans creation of the Image Builder appliance instance on the remote.

nodes.name: Provision builder
nodes.summary: Generates the provisioning script installing build tooling on the appliance.

nodes.name: Plan remote build & GHCR target
nodes.summary: Plans a remote image build assembling the request and GHCR target.

nodes.name: Run remote build script
nodes.summary: Generates and runs the shell script that builds and publishes images.

nodes.name: Sync image into remote
nodes.summary: Plans syncing/importing the built image into the target remote.

nodes.name: Create Storage Share
nodes.summary: Creates a Storage Share from a source project to recipient tenants: builds the share create command, plans the share, enforces caller tenant access, creates it via the Auth App API, and reconciles recipient mounts.

nodes.name: Build share create command
nodes.summary: Builds the CLI command creating a tenant share.

nodes.name: Plan storage share
nodes.summary: Plans creation of a Storage Share from a source project to recipient tenants.

nodes.name: Enforce tenant access
nodes.summary: Middleware enforcing caller access to the target tenant for share operations.

nodes.name: Create share via API
nodes.summary: API handler that creates the new tenant share.

nodes.name: Reconcile recipient mounts
nodes.summary: Reconciles the share's recipient mounts into recipient tenants.

nodes.name: Broker Provision Tenant/Project
nodes.summary: The mTLS Project Broker provisions tenants and projects: it dispatches the HTTP request, resolves the principal from the certificate fingerprint, authorizes admin/project access, and provisions the tenant or a project within it.

nodes.name: Dispatch broker request
nodes.summary: Dispatches the broker HTTP request, extracting the caller's certificate fingerprint.

nodes.name: Resolve principal
nodes.summary: Resolves the certificate fingerprint into a broker principal via the trust mapper.

nodes.name: Authorize access
nodes.summary: Determines whether the caller has admin/project privileges on the remote.

nodes.name: Provision tenant
nodes.summary: Provisions a full tenant on behalf of the project broker via v2 creation.

nodes.name: Create project in tenant
nodes.summary: Creates a project within an existing tenant on behalf of the broker.

edges.description: On first CLI device login the Auth App provisions the user's personal tenant.
The mTLS Project Broker and operator install provision tenants and their projects.
Tenant creation brings up the sidecar's Tailscale and split-DNS.
Machines obtain short-lived Workload Identity Tokens from the Sandcastle OIDC Provider.
Machine connectivity depends on the tailnet, split-DNS and host overrides.
Public routes are applied through the shared Caddy provisioned by infrastructure.
Route plans resolve the target machine to publish.
